nodes.context: Chechnya-based detachments of Russian state agencies, described as Kadyrov's private army.
Former separatist government of Chechnya; its militants were pardoned and joined Kadyrovites.
Chechen militant unit loyal to Kadyrov, active in the Russo-Ukrainian War since 2014.
Chechen militant unit loyal to Kadyrov, active in the Russo-Ukrainian War since 2014.
Ukrainian National Guard unit based in Mariupol, involved in conflict with Chechen troops.
Separatist entity in eastern Ukraine; its conscripted soldiers were executed by Chechen troops.
Chechnya-based detachments of Russian National Guard, Ministry of Internal Affairs, and Ministry of Defence, described as Kadyrov's private army.
Chechen unit headed by Adam Delimkhanov, involved in kidnappings.
Russian federal security service, involved in operations with Kadyrovites.
Spetsnaz unit of the FSB headed by Movladi Baisarov, formally part of Kadyrovites.
Spetsnaz GRU unit headed by Said-Magomed Kakiev.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2015 BBC reported that a considerable part of Kadyrovites were Chechen Republic of Ichkeria militants pardoned by Russian president Vladimir Putin under the word of Ramzan Kadyrov
edges.context: Kadyrovites were formed from pardoned former militants of the Chechen Republic of Ichkeria.

edges.evidence_quote: Chechen militants loyal to Kadyrov have been active in the Russo-Ukrainian War since 2014. These forces include the Vostok Battalion and the Chechen Death Battalion.
edges.context: Vostok Battalion is a Chechen militant unit loyal to Kadyrov, part of Kadyrovite forces in Ukraine.

edges.evidence_quote: Chechen militants loyal to Kadyrov have been active in the Russo-Ukrainian War since 2014. These forces include the Vostok Battalion and the Chechen Death Battalion.
edges.context: Chechen Death Battalion is a Chechen militant unit loyal to Kadyrov, part of Kadyrovite forces in Ukraine.

edges.evidence_quote: The National Guard of Ukraine released a video appearing to show fighters from the Azov Regiment, based in Mariupol, greasing bullets in lard (salo) to be used against Chechen troops as an insult
edges.context: Azov Regiment fighters prepared bullets to be used against Chechen troops, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: Ukrainian media reported that Chechen troops executed conscripted soldiers of the Luhansk People's Republic who refused to fight.
edges.context: Kadyrovite troops executed conscripted soldiers of the Luhansk People's Republic.

edges.evidence_quote: On 17 June 2006, a group of the Kadyrovites and FSB officers killed the president of Ichkeria, Abdul Halim Sadulayev
edges.context: Kadyrovites and FSB jointly killed a rebel leader.

edges.evidence_quote: The Goretz (Mountaineer) detachment of the Kadyrovites was a spetsnaz unit of the FSB headed by Movladi Baisarov
edges.context: Goretz was a detachment within the Kadyrovites.

edges.evidence_quote: The Kadyrovites ended up backing down in that confrontation when another Chechen warlord, Said-Magomed Kakiev, head of the Spetsnaz GRU unit the Special Battalion Zapad, came down on Baisarov's side.
edges.context: Special Battalion Zapad opposed Kadyrovites during a confrontation.

edges.evidence_quote: According to some sources, Umarov's father, Khamad Umarov, was kidnapped back on 5 May 2008, by the Kadyrovite employees of the Oil Regiment (Neftepolk) headed by Chechnya's First Deputy Prime Minister Adam Delimkhanov.
edges.context: Oil Regiment employees are Kadyrovites, indicating Oil Regiment is part of Kadyrovites.
